items.title: BN announces Johor election candidate lists, Onn Hafiz spearheading the charge
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 24 — Barisan Nasional (BN) announced its list of 56 candidates for the Johor election today, ending speculation over which candidates the coalition would field. Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the list of candidates for the coalition that will contest in the upcoming Johor election. Present were respective component party and affiliate group leaders such as MCA president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong; MIC president Tan Sri S.A. Vigneswaran; People’s Progressive Party (PPP) president Datuk Dr J. Loga Bala Mohan and Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh.

items.title: 'No problem' fielding non-Malay candidate in Permas, says Mat Sabu
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Amanah president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu has defended the party's decision to field Sharon Teo Siew Hui in Permas for the Johor election despite a boycott by the party's Pasir Gudang division. He was responding to questions on the decision by Amanah Pasir Gudang to boycott Teo's candidacy. Earlier, Amanah Pasir Gudang announced it would boycott the Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for Permas , who was named by PH chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim on June 22.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 24, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The FBM KLCI closed higher today as bargain hunting activities emerged following the recent market weakness. In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin traded higher at RM260,733. That wraps up today's Market Pulse.

items.title: Indonesia offers US$121 bil investment opportunities for developing EV battery ecosystem
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia is offering investment opportunities worth US$121 billion (US$1 = RM4.14) to develop its national electric vehicle (EV) battery manufacturing ecosystem, as the country seeks to strengthen its position in the global battery supply chain. Indonesia's Ministry of Investment and Downstreaming Director of Downstreaming Strategy and Governance Ahmad Faisal Suralaga said the opportunities cover the entire EV battery value chain, from mineral processing and battery materials to battery and electric vehicle manufacturing. "By 2045, Indonesia intends to become one of the top five EV battery producers in the world.

items.title: MMEA detains three foreign workers at fish cage off Teluk Bahang
items.summary: PENANG: The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) detained three foreign workers at a fish cage off Teluk Bahang here yesterday. Penang maritime director Captain Muhammad Suffi Mohd Ramli said the operation was carried out about 1pm following intelligence received by the state maritime operations centre. "Checks revealed that three foreign workers failed to produce valid work permits.

items.title: Ringgit extends gains on strong Malaysian economic data
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit extended its gains to close higher against the US dollar and other major currencies, as well as ASEAN peers, on Wednesday, supported by stronger-than-expected economic data in Malaysia, which boosted investor confidence and market sentiment. According to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM), Malaysia's foreign direct investment (FDI) rose 41.2 per cent to RM65.9 billion in 2025 from RM46.7 billion in 2024, driven mainly by equity injections from foreign investors and inflows into debt instruments. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the encouraging economic data continued to support the ringgit, reflecting growing confidence in Malaysia's economic outlook.

items.title: KL cable theft: Cops record major drop in cases
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Cable theft cases in the capital have declined significantly, with 71 reports recorded between January and May this year compared with 205 cases throughout 2025. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said police remained committed to combating cable theft through continuous enforcement operations across the city. He said 171 suspects were arrested in connection with cable theft cases last year involving telecommunications cables, railway infrastructure and other cable-related offences.

items.title: Armed Forces Cooperative declares 7.3 pct dividend, pays out RM126.2 mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Koperasi Angkatan Tentera Malaysia Bhd (Koperasi Tentera) has declared a 7.3 per cent dividend involving a total payout of RM126.2 million to more than 154,000 members for the financial year ended 2025. He said Koperasi Tentera recorded a net income of RM278 million for the financial year ended 2025, up from RM253 million in 2024. "I am proud of Koperasi Tentera's achievement in maintaining its status as a premier cooperative with assets exceeding RM3 billion.

items.title: Not out to get you: New Section 42A meant to rein in illegal racing, dangerous speed trials
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke has clarified that the new Section 42A under the Road Transport (Amendment) Bill 2026 is not intended to penalise isolated speeding or ordinary overtaking. Instead, he explained that it targets illegal racing and dangerous speed trials involving competitive conduct. “It targets racing or speed trials involving competitive conduct, challenge based speeding, reckless behaviour, or situations that clearly indicate dangerous activity.

items.title: Dr Adham Baba, ex-Bersatu rep among Johor BN candidates
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Former Endau assemblyman Alwiyah Talib and former health minister Datuk Seri Dr Adham Baba were among the 56 Johor Barisan Nasional candidates who will contest in the state election. In the 2022 state election, Alwiyah retained the seat when she polled 8,433 votes to defeat four other candidates. Onn Hafiz also announced that Dr Adham is the BN candidate for Pasir Raja.

items.title: High Court bars Papagomo from posting defamatory content against MCMC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court has granted an ex-parte interim injunction barring blogger Wan Muhammad Azri Wan Deris, better known as Papagomo, from publishing defamatory statements against the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) or disseminating harmful content online. In a statement today, MCMC said the injunction also restrains Wan Muhammad Azri from using any social media platform, whether through his existing accounts or newly created ones, to publish or spread such content. The report concerned a court case involving Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil and Wan Muhammad Azri.

items.title: Pakistan court sentences Balochistan rights activist to life in jail
items.summary: QUETTA: A Pakistani court has sentenced a prominent rights activist from southern Balochistan province to life imprisonment on murder and terror charges, drawing condemnation from civil liberties advocates this week. Baloch, the founder of the Baloch Yakjehti Committee (BYC), was handed the sentence in an anti-terrorism court in provincial capital Quetta on Monday, according to a copy of the decision. Baloch's legal team denied the charges, and her sister Nadia Baloch told AFP they would appeal the decision in a higher court.

items.title: Hilux driver escapes police chase by swerving into oncoming traffic
items.summary: Ampang Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Khairul Anuar Khalid said the incident began around 3.16am when a Toyota Hilux driver fled a roadblock mounted by Cheras police at the Billion Roundabout. A motorcycle officer from the Cheras police station's Crime Prevention Unit pursued the vehicle as it sped along the Middle Ring Road 2 towards Jalan Pandan Utama. Two multi-purpose vehicle (MPV) patrol units from the Pandan Indah police station joined the pursuit after receiving a request for assistance from the officer.

items.title: Keyfield bags RM229mil charter contracts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Keyfield International Bhd has secured 13 charter contracts worth RM229 million across Malaysia, Thailand and the Middle East. The offshore vessel provider said the contracts, which also include extension options worth RM41 million, further diversify Keyfield's international customer base and reinforce its position as one of Malaysia's leading offshore vessel owners and operators. Group chief executive officer and executive director Datuk Darren Kee Chit Huei said the contracts involve a diverse mix of vessel categories within its fleet as well as third-party vessels.

items.title: Keiko Fujimori: Peru's biggest political loser poised to win election
items.summary: PERU'S Keiko Fujimori is a perennial political loser. But on this, her fourth attempt, the daughter of disgraced late president Alberto Fujimori is on the verge of winning. Fujimori's surname is recognised across the Andean nation, which has had eight presidents since 2016.

items.title: Civil servants drive Malaysia's rise in global competitiveness, says Anwar
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH: Malaysia's improved performance in the 2026 World Competitiveness Ranking by the International Institute for Management Development (IMD) is largely due to the efficiency and commitment of the country's civil service, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. Anwar praised civil servants, describing them as the backbone of the achievement, which has also drawn recognition from several world leaders, including Turkmenistan President Serdar Berdimuhamedov during his recent visit to the country. Also present were Melaka Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, and Public Service Department director-general Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abd Aziz.

items.title: Johor election: BN unveils full candidate line-up
items.summary: Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the candidate line-up at the Johor Umno headquarters here. Buloh Kasap - Datuk Zahari Sarip N2. Tenang - Mohd Azahar Ibrahim N6.

items.title: Immigration raid uncovers filthy, overcrowded workers' hostel in Port Klang
items.summary: PORT KLANG: A filthy and poorly maintained hostel believed to be housing foreign workers in cramped conditions was uncovered during a large-scale immigration enforcement operation at an industrial premises here today. The two-storey building, located within a factory compound, was found to be dirty, foul-smelling and overcrowded when officers from the Immigration Department raided the premises under Ops Mega. Members of the media accompanying the operation observed dozens of foreign workers living in congested conditions inside the structure, which is believed to have been built without approval.

items.title: As traffic violations rise, so must the penalties: Loke defends RM500 fine hike
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke today defended his ministry’s push to raise the penalty for traffic violations to RM500, amid some pushback from Opposition lawmakers who said the move could disproportionately penalise lower income road users. MOT had proposed the increment as part of several amendments to the Road Transport Act tabled Monday, as Loke vowed to impose harsher penalties amid concerns over the rising cases of non-compliance and road safety. Loke explained that the authorities can still impose a lower fine for a violation on a case-to-case basis, countering allegations that the increment would lump lesser serious offences together with more serious violations.

items.title: [UPDATED] No coalition govt: Onn Hafiz says BN will govern alone if it wins Johor
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional (BN) Johor has ruled out forming a coalition government with any other political party after the Johor election, insisting its position is final and non-negotiable. Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the coalition's stance would remain unchanged regardless of the election outcome, reiterating that BN intends to govern on its own if given the mandate. "BN Johor will not form a coalition government with any other political party in this state," he said during the BN candidate announcement ceremony at the Johor Umno Liaison Office here today.

items.title: Starmer ally Jones backs Burnham after being reassured on economic plans
items.summary: LONDON: Darren Jones, an ally of outgoing Prime Minister Keir Starmer, endorsed Andy Burnham to take over on Wednesday after saying he had been reassured by the former mayor's economic plans, including ways he could possibly borrow more. Jones, chief secretary to Starmer, said he had discussed economic policy with Burnham after he alarmed investors last year by saying the country had to stop being in "hock to the bond markets", comments he later said had been misrepresented. Asked by Sky News if Burnham would borrow more, Jones said markets, trade unions and the governing Labour Party would accept targeted additional borrowing if backed by a credible plan to stimulate the economy.

items.title: Ministry hosts health screening for Media Prima staff in conjunction with Hawana
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Higher Education Ministry is strengthening cooperation with media practitioners through the Sihat Selalu #RakanMediaKPT programme, which offers comprehensive health screening services. The programme, held at Balai Berita Media Prima Bhd (Media Prima) here today in conjunction with National Journalists' Day (Hawana) 2026, involves medical specialists from the University Malaya Medical Centre (UMMC). Higher Education Ministry deputy secretary-general (policy) Ahmad Rizal Adnan said the initiative aims to promote a healthy lifestyle among media practitioners, who are often exposed to heavy workloads that may lead them to neglect self-care.

items.title: E-hailing driver fined RM101,750 for importing zam-zam water
items.summary: KLANG: An e-hailing driver was fined RM101,750 by the Magistrate's Court here today after being found guilty of importing prohibited goods, namely 5,500 bottles of zam-zam water, through West Port, Klang, five years ago. Syed Fazillah was also ordered to pay customs duty amounting to 20 per cent of the value of the goods, totalling RM2,035, to the government through the Customs Department under Section 119A of the Customs Act 1967. In addition, the court ordered all 5,500 bottles of zam-zam water to be forfeited to Customs under Section 127 of the same Act.

items.title: Port Klang raid: Foreign worker found hiding in rubbish container, 252 detained
items.summary: KLANG: A foreign worker was found hiding inside a rubbish container at a Port Klang furniture factory during a raid on illegal immigrants today. Selangor Immigration deputy director (Control) Mohamad Khusairi Kamarudin said 252 illegal immigrants were detained in the operation, which began at 8am. He said 520 foreign nationals were inspected, with 252 men aged between 18 and 50 detained for various immigration offences.

items.title: Singapore, Indonesia, Malaysia and Brunei face high risk of severe haze this year
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Singapore, Indonesia, Malaysia and Brunei face a high risk of severe haze this year due to hot and dry weather conditions, biofuel demand and economic pressures, a research institute said Wednesday. The Singapore Institute of International Affairs said it was the second time it had issued a red risk rating since launching its Haze Outlook report in 2019. Land use could also intensify as demand for biofuels rises due to energy supply disruptions.

items.title: E-hailing driver fined RM101,750 for importing 5,500 bottles of zam-zam water
items.summary: KLANG, June 24 — An e-hailing driver was fined RM101,750 by the Magistrate’s Court here today after he was found guilty of importing prohibited goods, namely 5,500 bottles of zam-zam water through West Port, Klang, five years ago. He was also ordered to pay customs duty amounting to 20 per cent of the goods’ value, totalling RM2,035, to the Government through the Royal Malaysian Customs Department (JKDM) under Section 119A of the Customs Act 1967. The court further ordered all 5,500 bottles of zam-zam water to be forfeited to JKDM under Section 127 of the same Act.

items.title: [UPDATED] Dewan Rakyat passes Road Transport (Amendment) Act 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Dewan Rakyat today passed the Road Transport (Amendment) Act 2026, paving the way for stronger regulatory and enforcement powers under the Road Transport Act 1987. Loke said the amendments were necessary in light of Malaysia's persistently high road fatality rate. In 2025, Malaysia recorded 6,537 deaths from road accidents.

items.title: Bersatu only objects to PCM's entry into PN, not Pejuang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu has dismissed reports suggesting that the party had objected to Parti Pejuang Tanah Air's (Pejuang) entry into Perikatan Nasional (PN). Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz, in dismissing the report published by a Malay daily, said the party only objected to Parti Cinta Malaysia's (PCM) entry into the opposition bloc. "Bersatu supports Pejuang's entry into PN and only objects to PCM's inclusion.

items.title: Malaysia’s rise in competitiveness index reflects civil service efficiency, says Anwar
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, June 24 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today praised the efficiency of the country’s civil service for contributing to Malaysia’s improved performance in the IMD World Competitiveness Index 2026. No, it is due to the system, the civil service and the entire team, the civil servants. The event was also attended by Melaka Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh, Deputy Minister of Higher Education Adam Adli Abd Halim, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Public Service director-general Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz, Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah and State Secretary Datuk Azhar Arshad.

items.title: Honda Jazz driver sent to Henry Gurney School for three years over 2025 crash that killed three teens
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — The Magistrates’ Court in Seremban today ordered the Honda Jazz driver who drove dangerously, causing the deaths of three teenagers in a crash at Matahari Heights last March, to be sent to Henry Gurney School for three years. Magistrate Nurul Azuin Mohd Talhah handed down the sentence after the young offender, now aged 18 years and nine months, pleaded guilty under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, Harian Metro reported. The court also ordered the teenager to be placed under the supervision of a moral officer for one year after completing detention.

items.title: KLIA drug crackdown nets 653kg of cannabis buds, 38 arrests this year
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 24 — Police have seized a total of 653.6 kilogrammes (kg) of cannabis buds at the Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) in the first six months of this year. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said this was the result of joint enforcement conducted by the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and KLIA security authorities in their ongoing efforts to tighten security measures at the country’s main entry and exit points through screening operations. Meanwhile, commenting on their latest crackdown at KLIA from June 17-22, Shazeli said police conducted three raids and seized cannabis buds weighing 171.6kg worth RM13.73 million and also arrested four individuals, including a Malaysian believed to be a drug mule.

items.title: Man pleads guilty to insulting, threatening posts targeting Terengganu royal family
items.summary: Azman Hakim Hassanuddin, 35, was charged under Section 233(1)(a) of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 with making and initiating the transmission of communications that were grossly offensive and threatening in nature through his X account on Feb 19. Under four of the charges, he was accused of posting communications that were grossly offensive in nature at 2.25 pm, 4.19 pm, 4.20 pm and 4.29 pm, with the intent to annoy others. For the remaining charge, Azman Hakim, who was unrepresented, was accused of posting a threatening communication at 3.46 pm with the intent to threaten another person.

items.title: No adverse effects on Iran despite 'pragmatists' vs 'hardliners' rivalry
items.summary: FACTIONAL divisions and opposition to talks with the United States exist within the Iranian hierarchy, but such splits will not suffice on their own to derail the negotiating process, analysts say. After five weeks of war paused by an April ceasefire and ended by an accord this month, US Vice President JD Vance and Iran's top negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf held talks in Switzerland mediated by Qatar and Pakistan to begin a process in search of a final agreement. "There are certainly factions seriously opposed to the talks and to any compromises with the US," said Yale University lecturer Arash Azizi.

items.title: Kedah Sultan visits Sultan Abdul Samad Building
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Sultan of Kedah, Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, today visited the Sultan Abdul Samad Building (BSAS), a heritage landmark that played a significant role in Malaysia's history. His Royal Highness, who arrived at about 10.30am, was received by Khazanah Nasional managing director Datuk Amirul Feisal Wan Zahir. Amirul Feisal said the royal visit was deeply appreciated and was significant to Khazanah Nasional's efforts to preserve the heritage building, which extend beyond conserving its physical structure to highlighting the history of Kuala Lumpur, Selangor and Malaysia's development.

items.title: Anwar: Elections should be between political parties, don’t involve royalty
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called for elections to be treated strictly as a contest between political parties and not to be linked in any way to the royal institution. He said this when commenting on allegations involving Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu, who was said to have made a veiled remark against the Regent of Johor (TMJ) Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim during a recent programme, Sinar Harian reported today. Anwar said he maintained good relations and understanding with TMJ and hoped all parties would avoid escalating tensions over the issue.

items.title: Johor Umno Youth reminds leaders to remain loyal ahead of state election
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Umno Youth today reminded party leaders to remain loyal to Umno and BN following concerns raised by Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi over the coalition's candidates for the upcoming state election. Its chief Noor Azleen Ambros said loyalty to the party should not be dependent on positions or personal interests, stressing that members must respect decisions made by the party leadership. Noor Azleen said Puad, as a senior party leader, should set a positive example for younger members by demonstrating his commitment to the party.

items.title: Trump orders probe as gas prices fail to fall fast enough
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Donald Trump said on Wednesday he ordered an investigation into major oil companies over high gasoline prices as the US leader faces criticism over the impact of the Mideast war. "The big Oil Companies are not dropping their price at the pump commensurate with the sharply lower prices they are paying for Oil," Trump wrote on Truth Social. "Those prices are dropping like a rock!

items.title: Man to face 207 charges for signing contracts as a 'director' while bankrupt
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL: A man is expected to be charged tomorrow after he allegedly acted as a company director and entered into multiple contracts while an undischarged bankrupt. The suspect is alleged to have signed six Letters of Award and issued 201 official company letters under the designation in the course of business dealings. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Court orders mental evaluation for man who pleaded guilty to royal insults online
items.summary: SEPANG: The Sessions Court today ordered a 37-year-old unemployed man to undergo psychiatric evaluation after he pleaded guilty to insulting and threatening members of the Terengganu royal family online. Judge Ahmad Fuad Othman directed Azman Hakim Hassanuddin to be assessed at Hospital Bahagia Ulu Kinta in Perak. Earlier, Azman Hakim admitted to uploading grossly offensive and menacing images as well as postings against the royal family.

items.title: In Sarawak, security guard jailed eight months, fined RM50,000 for offensive Facebook comment against King
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — A security guard was sentenced to eight months’ jail and fined RM50,000 by the Sessions Court in Kuching, Sarawak, today after pleading guilty to posting an offensive comment against the His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, via his Facebook account. Judge Iris Awen Jon handed down the sentence on Raden Mohamad Norhakim Raden Ibrahim, 46, and ordered him to serve an additional two months’ jail if he fails to pay the fine, Bernama reported. According to the charge, the accused was charged with making an offensive communication with intent to annoy others through a Facebook post on February 12 last year.

items.title: Malaysia, SE Asia on red alert for severe haze risk in 2026, warns SIIA report
items.summary: SINGAPORE, June 24 — Malaysia, Brunei, Indonesia and Singapore could face severe transboundary haze in the coming months as South-east Asia has been placed on red alert for an elevated haze risk, according to the Singapore Institute of International Affairs (SIIA). In its Haze Outlook 2026 report released on Wednesday, SIIA identified August and September as the peak risk period, with the threat compounded by the El Niño and Indian Ocean Dipole (IOD) weather phenomena. “Some meteorologists have also warned of a potential positive IOD, which could further intensify hot and dry weather conditions and increase fire risks across South-east Asia,” it said in a statement issued in conjunction with the report’s release.

items.title: Zara Qairina inquest: Expert says copying handwriting across multiple pages difficult, court hears
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 24 — The Coroner’s Court here today was told that copying another person’s handwriting over many pages would be difficult as differences in writing habits would usually appear. Dr Linthini Gannetion, a forensic document examiner and certified handwriting expert, said that although she would not describe such an act as impossible, it would be difficult because handwriting involves several individual features. Linthini said that before comparing disputed handwriting, an examiner would first identify repeated and unconscious writing habits within a person’s own handwriting using the method ACE-V, namely analyse, compare, evaluate and verify.

items.title: SMK Suria Jaya in Johor to open in September after receiving partial occupation certificate, says MOE
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, June 24 — Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Suria Jaya here is expected to commence operations in September after the Ministry of Education (MOE) handed over the school project under a Certificate of Partial Occupation (CPO) yesterday. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek expressed appreciation to the project management team, consultants, relevant government agencies, utility providers and contractors for their commitment in ensuring the successful completion of the school project. “God willing, the school will begin operations in September 2026.

items.title: RM1.7b NPE 2 to feature barrier-free MLFF toll system to ease Klang Valley congestion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — The New Pantai Expressway Extension Project (NPE 2) will use a barrier-free toll collection system or Multi-Lane Free Flow (MLFF) when fully completed to reduce traffic congestion, especially during peak hours. Chua said the highway extension project would strengthen the integrated highway network in the Klang Valley through better connectivity between the NPE, the Sungai Besi Expressway (BESRAYA) and the proposed Istana-Kiara Expressway (LIKE) in line with the aspirations of the Kuala Lumpur Traffic Master Plan 2040. Commenting on traffic management during the construction work, Chua said a comprehensive Traffic Management Plan (TMP) would be implemented throughout the period to ensure smooth traffic flow and safety of road users.

items.title: SARA programme expanded to include more local products, says finance minister II
items.summary: KUCHING, June 24 — The government continues to strengthen the implementation of the Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (SARA) programme by opening up opportunities for more products from local entrepreneurs to be marketed based on consumer needs and preferences in a given area. Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan stated that this approach not only benefits aid recipients but also helps local entrepreneurs and retailers generate income. “In Kuching, there are local products that are popular among the local community.

items.title: Online scam losses surge to RM2.97b in 2025, says Home Ministry as fraud trend worsens across Malaysia
items.summary: The Home Ministry (KDN) said according to the comparison of various modus operandi, non-existent investments consistently recorded the largest losses with a record of RM848.62 million in 2024, jumping to RM1.46 billion in 2025 and RM361.63 million this year. According to KDN, the trend was closely followed by telecommunications crime which recorded RM497.12 million (2024), RM802.47 million (2025) and RM235.63 million (as of May 2026), while love scams continued to record losses of RM45.87 million (2024), RM47.44 million (2025) and RM17.76 million for the early period of this year. From a geographical aspect, he said Selangor and Kuala Lumpur continued to record the highest losses, with Selangor jumping from RM446.16 million (2024) to RM986.79 million (2025), while Kuala Lumpur increased from RM293.30 million (2024) to RM782.86 million (2025).

items.title: Stats Dept: Domestic tourism spending climbs 15.8pc to RM34b in Q1 2026 amid festive travel boost
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 24 — Domestic tourism expenditure recorded a double-digit growth of 15.8 per cent year-on-year to RM34 billion in the first quarter of 2026 (1Q 2026), according to the Bulletin of Malaysia Domestic Tourism Survey - First Quarter 2026 released by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) today. In a statement, DOSM said Malaysia’s domestic tourism maintained its growth momentum in the first three months of 2026, with total domestic visitors reaching 74.7 million, reflecting a year-on-year increase of 7.2 per cent. The performance was further supported by a 0.9 per cent quarter-on-quarter growth compared to the fourth quarter of 2025, indicating continued stability in domestic travel demand.

items.title: MRCB secures consent judgment in Shah Alam Stadium suit; case against blogger to continue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Malaysian Resources Corporation Bhd (MRCB) has secured a consent judgment against one of the defendants in its lawsuit alleging a conspiracy to cause economic damage through online posts about the demolition and redevelopment of the Shah Alam Stadium. According to a statement, the consent judgment was entered at the Shah Alam High Court on June 22 against self-described activist Abdul Razak Ismail, one of two defendants named in the suit. MRCB had sued Abdul Razak and blogger Murray Hunter over a series of posts published on Facebook and Hunter’s Substack platform that questioned the stadium demolition and redevelopment project.

items.title: Major resurfacing works for pothole-hit Jalan Lahat to start next month
items.summary: IPOH, June 24 — Road users can expect smoother, safer journeys soon as a major resurfacing and pothole repair project is set to begin along the heavily trafficked Jalan Lahat here. Menglembu assemblyman Steven Chaw said the RM2.6 million project, funded through the Malaysian Road Records Information System (MARRIS) allocation, will cover approximately 1.9km in each direction, bringing the total resurfacing length to nearly 4km. Thousands of motorists travelling to and from Ipoh use this road every day.

items.title: Govt to assess Budi Diesel quota requests using actual consumption data, says minister
items.summary: Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said any decision regarding the programme, including the issue of increasing quotas, would be made based on actual usage data. “When we first started implementing the RON95 subsidy, some said the quota was insufficient. Amir Hamzah said previous experience with targeted subsidy programmes, including those for the e-hailing sector, showed that the government is always prepared to make adjustments based on actual usage data.

items.title: Education Ministry preparing for larger 2027 Year One intake with new classrooms and teacher recruitment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) is stepping up preparations for the intake of two Year One cohorts in 2027, including the construction of 2,596 new classrooms and the recruitment of 3,150 contract of service (COS) teachers, based on current registration figures. Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said the ministry has received 73,386 Year One registration applications for six-year-old children for the 2027 school session, while the number of seven-year-old children stands at 405,033. He said the combined total of 478,419 applications represents a 12.07 per cent increase compared with Year One enrolment in 2026, which involved only the seven-year-old cohort.

items.title: Fahmi: Social media age checks aimed at keeping under-16s off platforms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — The Child Protection Code (CPC) establishes a safe, practical and responsible age-verification mechanism to ensure only eligible users are allowed access to social media platforms. He said both codes aim to create a safer online environment, particularly by protecting children from online risks and harm. “Under the CPC, licensed service providers offering social media services are required to implement age-verification mechanisms rather than identity verification to ensure compliance with the minimum age requirement.

items.title: Business closures, downsizing led to 42,807 job losses this year, says Ramanan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — A total of 42,807 workers nationwide were reported to have lost their jobs from January to June 12 this year, said Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan. He said based on statistics from the Social Security Organisation (Socso), business closures and company downsizing were identified as the main causes of retrenchment, affecting 17,485 people or 40.85 per cent. “Kuala Lumpur recorded the highest number of job losses at 30 per cent or 12,844 people, followed by Selangor with 12,360 people, and Johor, which recorded a job loss rate of 8.1 per cent or 3,468 people,” he said during the Question Time at the Dewan Rakyat today.

items.title: National Unity Ministry unveils plans to transform Malaysia’s museum sector
items.summary: “In addition, the Madani Government has approved the development of the Malaysia Natural History Museum in Putrajaya, thereby realising an aspiration planned since 1989,” he said at the opening ceremony of the National-Level International Museum Day Festival 2026 at Padang Akasia, Angsana Johor Bahru Mall, today. He said the development of the natural history museum is among the strategic initiatives aimed at strengthening preservation and education related to biodiversity and Malaysia’s natural heritage. “To date, the Natural History Museum houses a total of 76,666 specimens, comprising 73,934 zoological collections, 2,075 botanical collections, and 657 geological collections.

items.title: Perkeso recorded over 96pc compliance in claims processing last year, says Ramanan
items.summary: He said a two-day processing period was set for Funeral Benefit and Temporary Disablement Benefit claims, while Permanent Disablement Benefit, Invalidity Pension, Survivor’s Pension and Dependant’s Benefit claims are processed within three days. Mohd Isam wanted to know the official processing period for Perkeso claims, compliance levels and the use of digitalisation to expedite claims for contributors. Ramanan said Perkeso continuously enhances its operational systems through digitalisation, including the LINDUNG Faedah Perkeso portal, to improve claims processing and benefit payments.

items.title: Anthony Loke urges Malaysia to take lead in drones, air taxis and emerging aviation sector
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Malaysia must position itself as an active participant in the rapidly emerging low altitude economy (LAE) sector, which could potentially be the new growth engine, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said today. He said Malaysia has a solid base in traditional aviation and aerospace industries, but has not leveraged opportunities for maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) and manufacturing in the LAE sector. But for LAE, the standards and frameworks are being developed globally, and Malaysia must take this opportunity to write our own frameworks and standards,” he said at the inaugural Low Altitude Economy Forum 2026 here today.

items.title: Act 792 Amendment Bill seeks to extend coverage to child sexual offences committed abroad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — The Bill to amend the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017 (Act 792) seeks to extend the application of the act to offences committed overseas by individuals with ties to Malaysia. According to the Bill’s text published on the Parliament website today, the amendment to section 3 of Act 792 provides that offences committed outside Malaysia are punishable under the act if they involve a child who is a Malaysian citizen or a permanent resident. The amendment further provides that the Act will also apply to sexual offences against children committed overseas by Malaysian citizens or permanent residents.

items.title: Personal dispute, financial issues believed to be motive in Bandar Sunway private college student murder, says Selangor top cop
items.summary: Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said initial investigations found that the suspect and victim are believed to have known each other, were in a relationship, and had been living together, according to Astro Awani. “At this early stage of investigation, police believe the motive is related to personal issues involving emotions and financial spending,” he said at a press conference at the Selangor police headquarters today. Media reports yesterday said the victim’s body was found with multiple injuries at a condominium unit.

items.title: Kedah exco to face court over higher education access issue linked to ‘backdoor’ admissions claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Kedah Higher Education Exco Haim Hilman Abdullah is set to face charges at the Seremban Magistrates’ Court on Thursday over issues related to higher education access raised previously. “I will attend the Seremban Magistrates’ Court on June 25, 2026 at 9am to face charges related to higher education access issues that I raised in defence of the rights of Malaysian children, especially from B40 and M40 families,” he said. The Higher Education Ministry had earlier said it viewed the allegations seriously and was considering legal action to defend the integrity of the country’s higher education system.

items.title: Malaysia records 42,807 job losses as 697,000 roles at risk from AI without urgent upskilling, says HR minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan has warned that up to 697,000 jobs in Malaysia could be significantly affected by artificial intelligence (AI), digitalisation and the green economy within the next three to five years if workers fail to upgrade their skills. “The findings show that 697,000 jobs are expected to be highly affected by AI, digitalisation and the green economy if workers do not upskill within the next three to five years. Ramanan then said the government was prioritising reskilling and upskilling programmes, particularly for workers at risk of displacement due to technological change.
